REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS. TO MTN TO STRIKE PLTF'S VERIFIED COMPLAINT AND REQ. FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES FILED BY DEFENDANT SALK DPM, ROBERT S.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ROBERT SALK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS VERIFIED COMPLAINT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES - THE COURT GRANTS THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS TO THE POLICE AND DISTRICT ATTORNEY TRIGGER THE FIRST PRONG OF CCP 425.16. SEE SALMA V. CAPON (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 1275, 1285. SECTION 425.16, SUBDIVISION (E) DEFINES AN " 'ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF A PERSON'S RIGHT OF PETITION ...' " TO INCLUDE: "(1) ANY WRITTEN OR ORAL STATEMENT OR WRITING MADE BEFORE A LEGISLATIVE, EXECUTIVE, OR JUDICIAL PROCEEDING, OR ANY OTHER OFFICIAL PROCEEDING AUTHORIZED BY LAW; (2) ANY WRITTEN OR ORAL STATEMENT OR WRITING MADE IN CONNECTION WITH AN ISSUE UNDER CONSIDERATION OR REVIEW BY A LEGISLATIVE, EXECUTIVE, OR JUDICIAL BODY, OR ANY OTHER OFFICIAL PROCEEDING AUTHORIZED BY LAW ...." ( 425.16, SUBD. (E).) DEFENDANT'S COMMUNICATIONS MADE IN PREPARATION FOR OR IN ANTICIPATION OF THE BRINGING OF AN ACTION OR OTHER OFFICIAL PROCEEDING FALL WITHIN THE AMBIT OF THESE SUBDIVISIONS. PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON FLATELY V. MURO (2006) 39 CAL.4TH 299, IS MISPLACED. THE CRIMINAL EXCEPTION TO THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS APPLIES WHERE THE CRIMINAL CONDUCT IS CONCEDED OR CONCLUSIVELY ESTABLISHED. IN ADDITION, THE MERE VIOLATION OF A STATUTE DOES NOT MEET THE CRIMINAL ILLEGALITY REQUIREMENT. SEE FREMONT REORGNIZING CORP. V. FAIGIN (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 1153, 1169. HERE, DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS PRIVILEGED AND NOT CRIMINAL. SEE SHADDOX V. BERTANI (2003) 110 CAL.APP.4TH 1406, 1415-1417. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW THE DEPOSITION WILL CHANGE THE ANALYSIS. THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES SHOULD BE DONE THROUGH A NOTICED MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT SALK'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs is granted. Plaintiff failed to file a substantive opposition and the parties agree that defendant can recover $6,945.05 as reasonable fees and costs. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Continued To Feb-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

(First Appearance Fee Paid On)Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Verified Complaint And Request For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RODERICK P. BUSHNELL, TEL. NO. 217-3800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KEVIN M. SMITH OF THE BRADLEY CURLEY ASIANO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 464-8888 EXT. 323 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT SALK's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Verified Complaint And Request For Attorneys Fees - The Court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. Defendant's statements to the police and District Attorney trigger the first prong of CCP 425.16. See Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275, 1285. Section 425.16, subdivision (e) defines an " 'act in furtherance of a person's right of petition ...' " to include: "(1) any written or oral statement or writing made before a legislative, executive, or judicial proceeding, or any other official proceeding authorized by law; (2) any written or oral statement or writing made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by a legislative, executive, or judicial body, or any other official proceeding authorized by law ...." ( 425.16, subd. (e).) Defendant's communications made in preparation for or in anticipation of the bringing of an action or other official proceeding fall within the ambit of these subdivisions. Plaintiff's reliance on Flately v. Muro (2006) 39 Cal.4th 299, is misplaced. The criminal exception to the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis applies where the criminal conduct is conceded or conclusively established. In addition, the mere violation of a statute does not meet the criminal illegality requirement. See Fremont Reorgnizing Corp. v. Faigin (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1153, 1169. Here, defendant's conduct was privileged and not criminal. See Shaddox v. Bertani (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1406, 1415-1417. The Court denies the request for a continuance. Plaintiff fails to explain how the deposition will change the analysis. The Request for Attorneys Fees should be done through a noticed motion. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
